Care plan review is due. The current plan was last reviewed January 2026. A review is recommended given the verbal approval of gardening services in March and the upcoming Q2 period. Patricia Dunne will contact Robert to schedule.

The Allied Health Assistant programme is brokered through Western Sydney Allied Health. Their rate of $89.00/hr is consistent with Hillview's contracted brokered rate. However, Hillview's own published rate for equivalent services is $84.50/hr. The brokered rate premium is $4.50/hr.
Robert's oncology review at Nepean Hospital is ongoing. The care team should be advised of any changes to his treatment plan that may affect his care needs and classification.

anomalies.headline: Allied Health Assistant stream classification flagged for verification.
anomalies.detail: The first AHA-003 line item (5 March) carries flags questioning whether Allied Health Assistant services should be classified as Clinical or another stream. The original note states: 'Allied Health Assistant services may attract participant contribution depending on stream classification. Classified Clinical per care plan — verify with care manager.' This suggests uncertainty about the stream assignment. If reclassified to Independence or EverydayLiving, participant contribution would be due, affecting the $0.00 currently charged.
evidence: Line item 1 (5 Mar, AHA-003) flags: 'Allied Health Assistant services may attract participant contribution depending on stream classification. Classified Clinical per care plan — verify with care manager.'
anomalies.suggested_action: Contact the care manager (Patricia Dunne) to confirm that AHA-003 is correctly assigned to Clinical stream with 0% participant contribution. If the stream should be different, recalculate the participant contribution on all three delivered AHA-003 line items (05-Mar, 12-Mar, 19-Mar).

anomalies.headline: Brokered Allied Health Assistant rate premium of $4.50/hr charged across 4.5 delivery hours.
anomalies.detail: Provider notes explicitly disclose both rates: Hillview's published rate for equivalent services is $84.50/hr, while the brokered rate (Western Sydney Allied Health) is $89.00/hr. The premium is $4.50/hr. Three delivered AHA-003 line items (5 Mar, 12 Mar, 19 Mar) totalled 4.5 hours at the brokered rate of $89.00/hr. Dollar impact: (89.00 − 84.50) × 4.5 = $20.25.
evidence: Provider notes state: 'Hillview's own published rate for equivalent services is $84.50/hr. The brokered rate premium is $4.50/hr.'
Three non-cancelled AHA-003 line items at 1.5 hours each (5 Mar, 12 Mar, 19 Mar) = 4.5 total hours
anomalies.suggested_action: Ask your provider whether the brokered rate premium can be absorbed by the provider rather than your budget. Providers are not required to pass brokered rate premiums to participants.

anomalies.headline: Transport service charged on a date when participant was recorded as interstate.
anomalies.detail: TR-003 (Community Transport, Oncology Review) is charged on 20 March at $74.00. Provider notes explicitly state: 'Participant was noted as interstate 19-23 March per provider records.' The transport was apparently charged on 20 March, which falls within the participant's documented absence. Provider notes request verification: 'Verify whether this transport occurred or is a billing error.'
evidence: Line item TR-003 (20 Mar): charged $74.00 gross
Provider notes on TR-003: 'Participant was noted as interstate 19-23 March per provider records. Verify whether this transport occurred or is a billing error.'
anomalies.suggested_action: Confirm with Hillview whether the transport to Nepean Hospital on 20 March actually occurred or should be credited back. If the participant was interstate, this service should be cancelled and removed from the account.

anomalies.headline: Domestic Assistance service charged on a date when participant was recorded as interstate.
anomalies.detail: DA-001 (Domestic Assistance) is charged on 20 March at $143.00 gross (2.0 hours × $71.50). Provider notes state: 'Participant was interstate 19-23 March. Confirm service occurred.' This service falls within the documented absence period and requires verification that it was actually delivered.
evidence: Line item DA-001 (20 Mar): charged $143.00 gross
Provider notes on DA-001: 'NOTE: Domestic assistance charged 20 March. Participant was interstate 19-23 March. Confirm service occurred.'
anomalies.suggested_action: Confirm with Hillview whether the domestic assistance service on 20 March actually occurred. If the participant was interstate, this service should be cancelled and credited.

anomalies.headline: Social Support service charged on a date when participant was recorded as interstate.
anomalies.detail: SS-002 (Social Support) is charged on 21 March at $152.00 gross (2.0 hours × $76.00). Provider notes state: 'Participant was interstate 19-23 March. Confirm service occurred.' This falls within the documented interstate period and requires verification.
evidence: Line item SS-002 (21 Mar): charged $152.00 gross
Provider notes on SS-002: 'NOTE: Social Support charged 21 March. Participant was interstate 19-23 March. Confirm service occurred.'
anomalies.suggested_action: Confirm with Hillview whether the social support service on 21 March actually occurred. If the participant was interstate, this service should be cancelled and credited.

anomalies.headline: Gardening service charged without written care plan amendment.
anomalies.detail: GM-006 (Gardening) is charged on 28 March at $143.00. Provider notes explicitly state: 'This service is not listed in Robert's current care plan (reviewed January 2026). Care manager Patricia Dunne verbally approved this service on 25 March. Written care plan amendment has not yet been issued. This charge should not appear on a statement for a service not in the written care plan.' Services must be documented in the written care plan before billing. The verbal approval is not sufficient authorisation for a charge to the participant's account.
evidence: Line item GM-006 (28 Mar): charged $143.00 gross for 2.0 hours

Provider notes: 'This service is not listed in Robert's current care plan (reviewed January 2026). Care manager Patricia Dunne verbally approved this service on 25 March. Written care plan amendment has not yet been issued. This charge should not appear on a statement for a service not in the written care plan.'
anomalies.suggested_action: Request that Hillview remove the $143.00 gardening charge pending completion of a formal written care plan amendment. Do not pay this amount until the service has been formally approved in writing and documented in an updated care plan.

anomalies.headline: Allied Health Assistant service cancelled due to worker unavailability with no replacement offered.
anomalies.detail: AHA-003 is cancelled on 26 March. The flags note: 'worker unavailable — no replacement offered, no prior notice to participant.' This is a Statement of Rights issue affecting continuity of care and advance notification of changes.
evidence: Cancelled line item AHA-003 (26 Mar): flags state 'worker unavailable — no replacement offered, no prior notice to participant'
anomalies.suggested_action: Raise this with Hillview and your care manager. You have a right to continuity of care and advance notice of any changes to your support workers. Ask whether the cancelled service can be rescheduled or whether a replacement worker is available.

anomalies.headline: Personal Care worker substituted without advance notice.
anomalies.detail: PC-001 on 14 March shows a worker substitution from Amara S. to James O. The provider notes state: 'Amara on leave — replacement, no prior notice.' This violates the Statement of Rights regarding advance notice of changes to support workers.
evidence: Line item PC-001 (14 Mar) shows worker change to 'James O.' with provider notes: 'Amara on leave — replacement, no prior notice'
anomalies.suggested_action: Contact your care manager to ensure that you receive advance notice of any future worker changes. You have the right to know about changes to your support team beforehand.

anomalies.headline: February 2026 brokered rate overage corrected with $12.00 credit.
anomalies.detail: Adjustment ADJ-2026-02-04 corrects a February overbilling on Social Support (SS-002, 21 Feb). The brokered rate charged was $82.00/hr but Hillview's published rate is $76.00/hr. Credit for 2.0 hours × $6.00 difference = $12.00. The credit has been applied to government_paid (reducing Hillview's billings), not to participant_contribution.
evidence: Previous period adjustment ADJ-2026-02-04: 'February 2026 — Social Support (SS-002, 21-Feb) overbilling correction. Brokered rate $82.00/hr vs. Hillview published rate $76.00/hr. Credit for 2.0 hrs × $6.00 difference.'
anomalies.suggested_action: Confirm that this $12.00 credit has been fully applied. This is an informational flag; no action required unless the credit is not visible on your account.

anomalies.headline: Non-slip bathroom mat AT-HM commitment not claimed 31 days after approval.
anomalies.detail: Commitment ATHM-2026-0071 (Non-slip bathroom mat, supply) was approved 28 February 2026. As of 31 March (the statement period end), no amount has been claimed despite 31 days having passed. Amount remaining: $85.00. Expiry date: 27 February 2027. The item should be ordered, supplied, and claimed before the expiry date.
evidence: AT-HM commitment ATHM-2026-0071: approval_date 2026-02-28, amount_claimed 0.00, amount_remaining $85.00, expiry_date 2027-02-27, status 'approved'
anomalies.suggested_action: Follow up with your care manager to arrange delivery/installation of the non-slip bathroom mat before this commitment expires on 27 February 2027. Contact Hillview to confirm the delivery timeline.

anomalies.headline: Robert James Okafor ended the quarter with $640.70 unspent — about 13% of the quarterly budget.

anomalies.headline: Care plan review is due or upcoming
anomalies.detail: Care plan review is due. The current plan was last reviewed January 2026. A review is recommended given the verbal approval of gardening services in March and the upcoming Q2 period. Patricia Dunne will contact Robert to schedule. A care plan review is an opportunity to ensure services match current needs — particularly important if there have been recent health changes.
evidence: provider note: Care plan review is due. The current plan was last reviewed January 2026. A review is recommended given the verbal approval of gardening services in March and the upcoming Q2 period. Patricia Dunne will contact Robert to schedule.
anomalies.suggested_action: Confirm the review date with your care manager. Bring notes on any changes since the last review — new diagnoses, medication changes, falls, or changes in daily ability.
